page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores, providing 4 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio.

This instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute resources without the performance variability of shared-core models, making it a stable choice for standard enterprise workloads. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this instance suitable for general-purpose applications, including web serving, application servers, microservices, and small-scale database hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8475B processor featuring 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs at a 2.7 GHz clock speed. The instance provides 16.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on external storage options. Network performance is defined by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. With its dedicated CPU allocation and standard 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers predictable compute performance. It is designed for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application hosting, microservices, and light database operations.
